panel.title: Connected Devices

card.network-description: Reported by the phone connectivity plugin.
card.signal-description: No live cellular reading is available.

panel.send-file-picker: Pick file to send to device

quick-actions.title: Quick actions
quick-actions.description: Common phone tools without leaving the panel.
quick-actions.wireless-adb-description: Pair or reconnect adb for taps and key input.
quick-actions.browse-description: Open the phone storage in your file manager.
quick-actions.send-description: Push files directly to the active device.
quick-actions.find-description: Make the phone ring so you can find it.

remote.title: Live control
remote.description: Tap, drag, and use Android navigation directly from the panel.
remote.connected-description: KDE Connect is active. Quick actions and remote control stay in one place.

wireless-adb.disabled-tooltip: Wireless ADB is disabled in settings. Open details.
wireless-adb.missing-command-description: Set a Wireless ADB helper command in the plugin settings
wireless-adb.missing-pair-parameters-description: Enter the phone IP, pairing port, and pairing code
wireless-adb.missing-connect-parameters-description: Enter the phone IP and connect port
wireless-adb.dialog-description: Use Android's Wireless debugging screen to pair with a code, then connect to the adb port.
wireless-adb.cable-only-description: Disable Wireless ADB fallback and force USB-only scrcpy and adb input for this plugin.
wireless-adb.qr-section-description: On the phone, open Wireless debugging and choose Pair device with QR code, then scan this image.
wireless-adb.qr-placeholder: Tap Start QR to generate a pairing code.
wireless-adb.qr-helper-description: The plugin will wait for the scan, pair automatically, then connect ADB and save the resolved host and port.
wireless-adb.qr-footer-description: Leave this popup open until the phone finishes the scan.
wireless-adb.qr-waiting-description: Waiting for the phone to scan the QR code and publish its pairing service.
wireless-adb.qr-success-description: Wireless ADB paired and connected from the QR code.
wireless-adb.qr-generate-error-description: Failed to generate the Wireless ADB QR code.
wireless-adb.missing-qr-parameters-description: Generate a fresh Wireless ADB QR code and try again.
wireless-adb.pair-section-description: On the phone, open Wireless debugging and choose Pair device with pairing code.
wireless-adb.connect-section-description: Use the adb port shown on the phone after pairing.
wireless-adb.legacy-section-description: This runs the configured helper command, such as adb tcpip 5555, for older or already-authorized ADB flows.
wireless-adb.running-description: Keep this panel open until adb finishes.
wireless-adb.disabled-banner-description: Wireless tools stay visible here for reference, but they are disabled until Wireless ADB is re-enabled in plugin settings.

embedded-mirror.starting-description: Launching scrcpy and preparing the panel overlay.
embedded-mirror.missing-feed-description: The scrcpy window is not available yet.
embedded-mirror.syncing-description: Waiting for the scrcpy window so it can be aligned to the phone frame.
embedded-mirror.not-configured-description: Set an embedded scrcpy command in the plugin settings.
embedded-mirror.ready-description: Tap, drag, and use the Android navigation bar below.

scrcpy.running-description: Click the phone tile again to stop the session
scrcpy.not-configured-description: Set a scrcpy command in the plugin settings
scrcpy.missing-command-description: Set a scrcpy command in the plugin settings

panel.pair-needed-subtitle: KDE Connect reported this device as temporarily unpaired.
panel.pair-requested-subtitle: Approve the request on the phone to restore controls.
panel.pair-requested: Confirm the pairing request on the phone. The mirror and device actions will come back automatically after approval.
panel.pair-description: This device is temporarily reported as unpaired. Retry pairing here if KDE Connect did not recover on its own after reconnecting.
panel.pair-waiting: Waiting for the phone to accept the pairing request.

kdeconnect-error.no-devices: No device running KDE Connect found
kdeconnect-error.unavailable-title: kdeconnectd does not seem to be running!
kdeconnect-error.unavailable-desc: Make sure you've installed the KDE Connect Application on your system and that it has started the kdeconnectd daemon

busctl-error.unavailable-desc: Make sure busctl (part of systemd) is installed on your system

no-device-connected-hide.label: Hide if unavailable
no-device-connected-hide.description: Hide the bar button entirely if no device is connected

iconColor.label: Icon Color
iconColor.desc: Color of the icon in the bar

phone-action.label: Phone Click Action
phone-action.description: Choose whether the phone tile wakes the device or launches scrcpy

scrcpy-command.label: scrcpy Command
scrcpy-command.description: Shell command to run when the phone tile is clicked. Example: scrcpy -s <serial>

scrcpy-stop-on-close.label: Stop scrcpy on Panel Close
scrcpy-stop-on-close.description: Terminate the scrcpy process when this panel closes

embedded-mirror-enabled.label: Embed Mirror in Panel
embedded-mirror-enabled.description: Start scrcpy in no-window mode and render it inside the panel through a V4L2 loopback feed.

embedded-scrcpy-command.label: Embedded scrcpy Command
embedded-scrcpy-command.description: Base scrcpy command for the in-panel mirror. The plugin appends --no-window and the V4L2 sink automatically.

mirror-performance-preset.label: Mirror Performance Preset
mirror-performance-preset.description: Tune the embedded mirror for lower latency or higher quality. Existing explicit scrcpy flags still win.

embedded-video-device.label: Loopback Video Device
embedded-video-device.description: Path to the v4l2loopback device scrcpy writes to, for example /dev/video10.

embedded-video-label.label: Loopback Device Label
embedded-video-label.description: Camera name fragment used by Qt Multimedia to match that loopback feed, for example scrcpy-panel.

embedded-video-encoder.label: Pinned Video Encoder
embedded-video-encoder.description: Optional scrcpy encoder override for this phone, for example c2.qti.avc.encoder.

embedded-video-codec-options.label: Video Codec Options
embedded-video-codec-options.description: Optional scrcpy video codec options passed through to Android's encoder.

adb-device-serial.label: ADB Device Serial
adb-device-serial.description: Optional. Leave empty to use the default adb device, or the saved Wireless ADB host and port if wireless fallback is enabled.

wireless-adb-enabled.label: Enable Wireless ADB
wireless-adb-enabled.description: Show Wireless ADB tools and allow the plugin to reuse the saved phone IP and port. Disable this for cable-only ADB.

mirror-reduce-background-polling.label: Reduce Background Polling While Mirroring
mirror-reduce-background-polling.description: Slow KDE Connect refreshes while the embedded mirror is active to free a bit of headroom.

mirror-debug-overlay-enabled.label: Enable Mirror Debug Overlay
mirror-debug-overlay-enabled.description: Show a compact performance pill in the panel and append --print-fps to scrcpy for extra logs.

wireless-adb-command.label: ADB TCP/IP Helper Command
wireless-adb-command.description: Legacy helper command available inside the panel's Wireless ADB dialog. The default uses adb tcpip 5555 for an already authorized ADB device; it does not toggle Android's Wireless debugging switch.
